chosen_archetypes.rationale: PixelForge serves precision-focused digital artists who treat the browser canvas as a functional workspace. Data-Dense provides the keyboard-first architecture, real-time metric readouts, and dense, zero-distraction chrome. Swiss principles ensure the grid-driven layout, restrained palette, and structural clarity prevent visual noise, letting the canvas and its coordinates dominate. Together they create an instrument-grade interface where every pixel and shortcut carries operational weight.
chosen_archetypes.signature_interaction: Instant cursor coordinate snapping and tool-state feedback with <60ms DOM updates, reinforcing a tactile, responsive relationship between hand and canvas.

primary.usage: Active tool highlight, selection borders, primary focus rings

secondary.usage: Destructive actions, selection highlights, error states

accent.usage: Success states, positive metadata, export confirmation

panels.usage: Tool rail, panel dock, title bar, status bar surfaces

dim.usage: Inactive text, disabled UI, metadata, placeholders

rules.usage: Grid lines, dividers, ruler tick marks, structural boundaries

iteration_3_directives.viewport_clipping_fix: In `renderAll()`, set `displayCanvas.width/height` dynamically to `canvasWorkspace.clientWidth/Height`. Clear rect, then apply `ctx.setTransform(zoom, 0, 0, zoom, 18 + panX, 18 + panY)`. This maps document coordinates exactly to the visible viewport without clipping or hard cutoff edges at nonzero pan offsets.
iteration_3_directives.filter_visibility_fix: In `applyFilter()`, call `renderAll()` immediately after `ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0)`. This forces synchronous layer recomposition so filter effects are visibly applied without waiting for unrelated triggers.
iteration_3_directives.eyedropper_contamination_fix: Remove any JS-drawn checkerboard. Rely entirely on CSS `repeating-conic-gradient` on `.canvas-container`. `getColorAt()` must sample `displayCtx.getImageData()` but mask out non-pixel areas, or calculate coordinates relative to the active layer canvas directly to avoid DOM pixel sampling issues.
iteration_3_directives.brush_shortcut_fix: In `onKeyDown`, add handling for `'['` and `']'` to decrement/increment `state.brushSize` by 1, clamped 1–200. Update both the UI input field and the status bar readout synchronously.
iteration_3_directives.undo_snapshot_order_fix: In `newDocument()`, move `saveHistory('New Document')` to AFTER `state.layers.push(bgLayer)` and `state.activeLayerIndex` assignment. The initial undo must capture the initialized background layer, not a blank slate.
iteration_3_directives.flood_fill_performance_fix: Replace `queue.shift()` in `floodFill()` with an `Int32Array` head-pointer stack (`push: stack[sp++]=x/y; pop: sp-=2; cx=stack[sp]; cy=stack[sp+1];`). Ensures O(n) complexity and fills 800x600 canvas in <50ms without main-thread blocking.

components.description: Fixed top bar for document metadata and file operations
Fixed left rail for 11 drawing tools
Fixed right panel for Layers, Tool Options, History, and Filters
Central drawing workspace wrapper handling rulers, checkerboard, and canvas resize logic
Fixed bottom bar for real-time metrics and tool state
Icon-only action button for tools and panel controls
Dedicated panel for 7 pixel filters. Controls mirror Tool Options styling.
requirements: 7 controls: Brightness (−100→+100), Contrast (−100→+100), Blur (0–20px), Sharpen (0–100%), Grayscale (one-click), Invert (one-click), Sepia (one-click). Each click/input calls `saveHistory()` before modifying `state.activeLayer.data`.

components.description: Numeric input for brush size, synced with bracket shortcuts

meta.title: PixelForge
meta.description: Professional browser-native image editor with zero dependencies.
lifecycle_fixes: Auto-save check moved to top of init() before newDocument(). Restore skips newDocument; decline falls through.
Document JSON key standardized to 'documentName' across save/restore.

layout.grid_template:
"title title title" / auto 1fr auto
                "tool top dock" / auto 18px auto
                "tool canvas dock" / auto 1fr auto
                "tool left dock" / auto 18px auto
                "status status status" / auto 1fr auto

requirements: CSS grid inside wrapper: `grid-template-columns: 18px 1fr; grid-template-rows: 18px 1fr;` to fix `.canvas-area` flex-column bug (MIN-002).
Zoom/Pan via `displayCtx.setTransform(state.zoom, 0, 0, state.zoom, rulerW + state.panX, rulerH + state.panY)` on every render.
Checkerboard transparency applied via CSS `repeating-conic-gradient` on `.canvas-container`, NOT on pixel data.
Pointer events: `pointerdown/pointermove/pointerup` with `setPointerCapture`. Push history for ['brush','pencil','eraser','spray'] on pointerUp.
Selection overlay fix: `left = (minX * zoom + state.panX + 18)`, `top = (minY * zoom + state.panY + 18)`.
Magic Wand fix: `let minX = x, minY = y, maxX = x, maxY = y`; replace all `minXBounds` with `minX`.

primary_user_journey: launch -> tool_select -> layer_select -> draw -> filter_apply -> export

accessibility.color_contrast: All text meets WCAG AA (≥4.5:1 for body, ≥3:1 for large/UI). Dark mode surfaces maintain ≥3:1 contrast for icons and controls.
accessibility.focus_indicators: 2px solid outline using {colors.primary} with 2px offset on all interactive chrome elements. Visible on Tab and keyboard navigation.
accessibility.alt_text_strategy: Tool icons use aria-label for name+shortcut. Canvas uses role='img' with live region updates for selection dimensions.
accessibility.screen_reader: Status bar metrics announce via aria-live='polite' throttled to 200ms to prevent VO spam.
accessibility.touch_targets: All interactive elements enforce min 44x44px on mobile breakpoints.
